name: Camino al Castillejo Trail
name_original: Subida a la Sierra de las Barbas de Oro — circular desde Valdecaballeros
description: Hike the moderate 4.9-mile Ascent to the Sierra de las Barbas de Oro from Valdecaballeros, featuring reservoir views and wildlife.
region_intro: Hiking around Herrera Duque offers diverse landscapes within the La Siberia region, recognized as a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. The area is characterized by expansive reservoirs like Cíjara, García de Sola, and Orellana, fed by the Guadiana River, which create varied terrain. Mountain ranges such as Sierra de la Ubría and hills like Consolación Hill provide elevated perspectives, while Mediterranean forests, pine forests, and diverse flora line the trails. Best hiking trails around Herrera Duque The most popular hiking route is Herrera del Duque Castle and Sierra de la Ubría Loop, a 14.9 miles (24.0 km) trail that takes 6 hours 53 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers extensive panoramic views and diverse landscapes. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cíjara Reservoir Loop from Helechosa de los Montes, a moderate 8.7 miles (14.0 km) path. This trail provides panoramic views and showcases Mediterranean scenery around the reservoir. Local hikers also love the Guadiana Natural Trail: Villarta de los Montes Loop, a 2.2 miles (3.6 km) trail leading through riverside landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 2 minutes. Hiking around Herrera Duque is defined by expansive reservoirs, river valleys, and varied mountain ranges. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside walks to challenging mountain loops. The routes in Herrera Duque are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 10 reviews. Nearly 100 hikers have used komoot to explore Herrera Duque's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Herrera Duque?
A: Herrera Duque offers a selection of 11 hiking routes, catering to various preferences. You'll find 2 easy trails, 8 moderate options, and 1 challenging route for more experienced hikers.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the hiking trails in Herrera Duque?
A: The terrain in Herrera Duque is quite varied, reflecting its location within the La Siberia UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. You can expect paths winding through Mediterranean forests, pine forests, and eucalyptus groves, especially around the reservoirs. Trails also traverse mountain ranges and hills, offering elevated perspectives, and follow riverbanks like the Guadiana. Surfaces can range from well-maintained paths to more rugged, natural trails.

Q: What natural features or wildlife can I expect to see while hiking?
A: Herrera Duque is rich in natural beauty and wildlife. You'll encounter expansive reservoirs such as Cíjara, García de Sola, and Orellana, along with the prominent Guadiana River. The region boasts diverse vegetation, including thyme, broom, and elm trees. It's also a haven for wildlife, with opportunities to spot various bird species like griffon vultures, black storks, and golden eagles, especially around the García de Sola Reservoir. Mammals such as deer, wild boars, rabbits, and foxes are also present, particularly during the rutting season from lookouts in the Cíjara Regional Hunting Reserve.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hikes in Herrera Duque?
A: Yes, Herrera Duque offers easy routes suitable for families. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1268672662/guadiana-natural-trail-villarta-de-los-montes-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadiana Natural Trail: Villarta de los Montes Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 2.2 miles (3.6 km) long and leads through pleasant riverside landscapes. Another easy choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31428657" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Herrera del Duque Town Center – Herrera del Duque loop from Herrera del Duque&lt;/a&gt;, a 3.2-mile (5.1 km) walk.

Q: Are there challenging hikes for experienced hikers in the area?
A: For those seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1266866436/herrera-del-duque-castle-and-sierra-de-la-ubria-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Herrera del Duque Castle and Sierra de la Ubría Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 14.9-mile (24.0 km) route. This trail offers extensive panoramic views and diverse landscapes, making it a rewarding experience for seasoned hikers.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Herrera Duque?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Herrera Duque are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1266895570/cijara-reservoir-loop-from-helechosa-de-los-montes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cíjara Reservoir Loop from Helechosa de los Montes&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.7-mile (14.0 km) circular path offering panoramic views and Mediterranean scenery. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1268647637/sierra-de-la-ubria-loop-from-fuenlabrada-de-los-montes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de la Ubría Loop from Fuenlabrada de los Montes&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 10-mile (16.1 km) loop.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Herrera Duque?
A: The hiking routes in Herrera Duque are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 10 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the extensive panoramic views from elevated points like the Sierra de la Ubría, and the serene Mediterranean scenery around the reservoirs.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or landmarks worth visiting along the trails?
A: Absolutely. The region is dotted with scenic viewpoints. Notable ones include the Castle Pass lookout, offering views of Herrera del Duque and its surroundings, and the Consolación Pass Lookout. The Navas Mountain Pass is also known for its breathtaking vistas. You can also explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5667292" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Herrera del Duque Town Center&lt;/a&gt; or visit nearby settlements like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5335527" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Helechosa de los Montes&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Herrera Duque?
A: The Pelochejo Creek route is ideally explored in spring or fall, suggesting these seasons offer pleasant conditions for hiking. Generally, spring and autumn provide milder temperatures, making them excellent times to enjoy the diverse flora and fauna without the intense heat of summer.

Q: Are there any trails that feature water bodies like rivers or reservoirs?
A: Yes, Herrera Duque is defined by its expansive reservoirs and rivers. Many routes wind around the Cíjara, García de Sola, and Orellana reservoirs, offering picturesque scenery. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1266895570/cijara-reservoir-loop-from-helechosa-de-los-montes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cíjara Reservoir Loop from Helechosa de los Montes&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example. You can also find trails along the Guadiana River, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1268672662/guadiana-natural-trail-villarta-de-los-montes-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadiana Natural Trail: Villarta de los Montes Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which showcases riverside landscapes.

Q: Are there any trails that offer views of historical sites or structures?
A: Yes, some trails provide views of historical elements. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1101999807/castillo-de-herrera-del-duque-from-herrera-del-duque-round-trip-via-sierra-del-chamorro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Herrera del Duque from Herrera del Duque – round trip via Sierra del Chamorro&lt;/a&gt; offers views of the castle. Additionally, the Navas Mountain Range Route highlights an ancient medieval bridge, adding a historical dimension to the natural landscape.
tips: Small town located between the foothills of the Sierra de las Barbas de Oro on one side and the Embalse de García Sola on the other. Some of their stork nests appear there, as it is a passage area.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this hike?
faqs.answer: The Ascent to the Sierra de las Barbas de Oro — circular from Valdecaballeros is estimated to take approximately 2 hours and 16 minutes to complete. This duration is for the full 7.88-kilometer circular route.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is rated as moderate, featuring generally accessible paths. However, hikers should be prepared for some uneven or rocky sections, particularly as it ascends into the foothills of the Sierra de las Barbas de Oro. Sure-footedness is recommended.

faqs.question: What kind of views and natural features can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: Starting from Valdecaballeros, the trail offers views of the Sierra de las Barbas de Oro mountain range and the expansive Embalse de García Sola (García Sola Reservoir). You'll pass through Mediterranean forests with holm oaks and cork oaks, characteristic of the Montes de Toledo mountain system.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter on this hike?
faqs.answer: The region is rich in biodiversity. Nature enthusiasts might spot mammals such as deer, wild boar, and otters. Birdwatching is a significant draw, with iconic species like imperial eagles, black vultures, and black storks inhabiting the area. Valdecaballeros is also known as a passage area for storks, and their nests can often be observed there.

faqs.question: Is the Ascent to the Sierra de las Barbas de Oro trail suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: Rated as moderate, this trail requires a good fitness level due to its length and elevation gain. While it's a circular route, it might be challenging for very young children or absolute beginners. Hikers with some experience and good physical condition will find it enjoyable.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead in Valdecaballeros?
faqs.answer: The trail starts from the town of Valdecaballeros. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, as a starting point for a popular hiking route, you should be able to find suitable parking within or on the outskirts of the town.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally accessible for dogs, but it's always recommended to keep them on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure their safety, especially given the diverse fauna in the La Siberia Extremeña region.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the Sierra de las Barbas de Oro?
faqs.answer: To avoid extreme heat, the best times to hike are typically spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is milder, and the natural landscape is often at its most vibrant, making for a more comfortable and scenic experience.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this hike?
faqs.answer: Based on available information, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Ascent to the Sierra de las Barbas de Oro — circular from Valdecaballeros trail.

faqs.question: What should I bring with me for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and length, it's advisable to bring plenty of water, appropriate hiking footwear for uneven terrain, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and snacks. Binoculars could enhance your wildlife and birdwatching experience.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants in Valdecaballeros after the hike?
faqs.answer: Yes, Valdecaballeros is a settlement, so you can expect to find local amenities such as cafes or bars where you can relax and refuel after completing your hike.
